title: A piece of advice you'd give your younger self
summary: Narrate the small moment behind the advice you would now give your younger self.
topic: A piece of advice you'd give your younger self
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) telling one specific piece of advice you would give a younger you, and the moment you wish you'd had it. Use the second conditional, past tenses for the memory, and at least one relative clause.
hintStarters: If I could speak to the version of me who…
What I didn't understand then was…

hintVocab.meaningVi: phiên bản trẻ hơn của mình
coi điều gì đó là nhằm vào mình

sampleText:
If I could speak to the version of me who walked out of an office in Edinburgh at twenty-three, crying in the rain because a manager had laughed at my pronunciation, I would not give her a pep talk. I'd sit down next to her on the wet bench, hand her a paper napkin from the café across the road, and tell her quietly that most people who make you feel small are not actually thinking about you. They are thinking about themselves, and you happened to be standing in front of them when their bad mood needed somewhere to go.

I took that laugh personally for nearly a year. I rehearsed the words I should have said. I avoided the manager who had made the joke, which meant I also avoided the parts of the job I actually liked. With hindsight, the moment was tiny: a busy person in an open-plan office, embarrassed by his own joke ten minutes later, who probably forgot it by lunch. The version of me on the bench didn't know any of that. She thought she had been seen and judged. I'd argue this is the most expensive mistake a young person makes — believing that a casual unkindness is a verdict. It isn't. It's usually weather. If she had understood that, she would have gone back inside, finished her project, and asked for a transfer when she was ready, not when she was bleeding.

Some people will say that pain is a teacher and that I should not soften the lesson. I disagree. The lesson would have arrived faster, and softer, if anyone had simply said, 'It's not about you.' I'm not asking my younger self to feel nothing; I'm asking her to feel the right thing. Embarrassment lasts about ten minutes if you let it. Shame, the kind we carry for months, is what happens when we mistake one rude moment for a permanent record. If I had a single sentence to leave on that bench, it would be this: be quick to feel and slow to decide what the feeling means. I think she would have looked up, blown her nose, and gone back inside before her coffee got cold.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: khiến bạn cảm thấy nhỏ bé
coi tiếng cười đó là nhằm vào mình
tập đi tập lại lời nói
một sự thiếu tử tế tình cờ
cảm thấy đúng điều cần cảm

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Second conditional with 'could'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'If I could speak to the version of me…, I would not give her a pep talk' dùng điều kiện loại 2 kết hợp với modal 'could' để mô tả tình huống giả định trong hiện tại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'If I could speak to the version of me…, I would not give her a pep talk' uses the second conditional with modal 'could' for a hypothetical present situation.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Third conditional for past hypothetical
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'If she had understood that, she would have gone back inside…' dùng câu điều kiện loại 3 để diễn tả điều đã không xảy ra trong quá khứ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'If she had understood that, she would have gone back inside…' uses the third conditional to describe something that did not happen in the past.

sampleTranslationVi:
Nếu tôi có thể nói chuyện với phiên bản của tôi đã bước ra khỏi một văn phòng ở Edinburgh khi hai mươi ba tuổi, khóc trong cơn mưa vì một người quản lý đã cười phát âm của tôi, tôi sẽ không đưa cho cô ấy một bài động viên. Tôi sẽ ngồi xuống bên cô ấy trên băng ghế ướt, đưa cho cô ấy một tờ khăn giấy từ quán cà phê đối diện, và nói khẽ với cô ấy rằng phần lớn những người khiến bạn cảm thấy nhỏ bé thực ra không nghĩ về bạn. Họ đang nghĩ về chính họ, và bạn tình cờ đứng trước mặt họ khi tâm trạng xấu của họ cần một chỗ để xả ra.

Tôi đã coi tiếng cười đó là nhằm vào mình trong gần một năm. Tôi tập đi tập lại những lời lẽ ra tôi nên nói. Tôi tránh người quản lý đã đùa câu đó, đồng nghĩa với việc tôi cũng tránh những phần của công việc mà tôi thực sự thích. Nhìn lại, khoảnh khắc đó nhỏ xíu: một người bận rộn trong một văn phòng mở, mười phút sau ngại ngùng vì chính câu đùa của mình, người có lẽ đã quên nó trước giờ ăn trưa. Phiên bản của tôi trên băng ghế không biết điều đó. Cô ấy nghĩ mình đã bị nhìn thấy và bị phán xét. Tôi cho rằng đây là sai lầm đắt giá nhất mà một người trẻ phạm phải — tin rằng một sự thiếu tử tế tình cờ là một phán quyết. Không phải vậy. Nó thường là thời tiết. Nếu cô ấy đã hiểu điều đó, cô ấy đã quay lại bên trong, hoàn thành dự án, và xin chuyển công tác khi đã sẵn sàng, không phải khi đang chảy máu.

Một số người sẽ nói rằng đau khổ là một người thầy và rằng tôi không nên làm mềm bài học. Tôi không đồng ý. Bài học đã đến nhanh hơn, và mềm hơn, nếu có ai đó chỉ đơn giản nói, 'Không phải về bạn đâu.' Tôi không yêu cầu phiên bản trẻ hơn của mình cảm thấy không gì cả; tôi yêu cầu cô ấy cảm thấy đúng điều cần cảm. Sự xấu hổ kéo dài khoảng mười phút nếu bạn cho phép nó. Sự nhục nhã, kiểu chúng ta mang theo hàng tháng, là điều xảy ra khi chúng ta nhầm một khoảnh khắc thô lỗ thành một hồ sơ vĩnh viễn. Nếu tôi có một câu duy nhất để lại trên băng ghế đó, nó sẽ là: hãy nhanh để cảm và chậm để quyết định cảm xúc đó có nghĩa gì. Tôi nghĩ cô ấy sẽ ngước lên, xì mũi, và quay lại bên trong trước khi cà phê của cô nguội.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly forms the second conditional?
options: If I can speak to the version of me, I would not give her a pep talk.
If I could speak to the version of me, I will not give her a pep talk.
If I could speak to the version of me, I would not give her a pep talk.
If I would speak to the version of me, I would not give her a pep talk.
mcQuestions.explanation: Second conditional with modal: 'If + could + base verb' in the if-clause; 'would + base verb' in the main clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is a correct third conditional?
options: If she would have understood that, she had gone back inside.
If she understood that, she would gone back inside.
If she has understood that, she will have gone back inside.
If she had understood that, she would have gone back inside.
mcQuestions.explanation: Third conditional = 'had + past participle' in the if-clause + 'would have + past participle' in the main clause.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Had' + past participle, then 'would have' + past participle.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option has the correct relative clause?
options: I avoided the manager who had made the joke, which meant I also avoided the parts of the job I actually liked.
I avoided the manager which had made the joke, that meant I avoided the parts of the job.
I avoided the manager who had made the joke, what meant I also avoided the parts I liked.
I avoided the manager that he had made the joke, which meant I avoided parts I liked.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Who' for the manager, 'which' for the whole previous clause; no double subject.
mcQuestions.hint: Person → 'who', referring to a whole clause → 'which'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows correct past simple usage for a single past action?
options: I taking that laugh personally for nearly a year.
I took that laugh personally for nearly a year.
I have took that laugh personally for nearly a year.
I am took that laugh personally for nearly a year.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Took' is the past simple of 'take', correct for a completed past situation with a duration.
mcQuestions.hint: Past simple for a completed past situation.

options: On hindsight, the moment was tiny.
In the hindsight, the moment was tiny.
With hindsight, the moment was tiny.
By hindsight, the moment was tiny.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural opinion phrase.
options: I argued this be the most expensive mistake young person make.
Argued I'd that this is the most expensive mistake makes a young person.
I'd argue this be the most expensive mistake making young person.
I'd argue this is the most expensive mistake a young person makes.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I'd argue + clause' is a polite, B1-appropriate opinion phrase.

options: I rehearsed the words I should have said.
I rehearsed the words I should had said.
I rehearsed the words I should have say.
I rehearsed the words I should saying.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Should have + past participle' expresses past regret or missed opportunity.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the modal 'would' correctly with a stative verb?
options: I think she would looked up, blown her nose, and gone back inside.
I think she would have looked up, blown her nose, and gone back inside before her coffee got cold.
I think she would has looked up, blew her nose, and gone back inside.
I think she will have looked up, blow her nose, and went back inside.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Would have + past participle' is the past hypothetical pattern, repeated in parallel verbs.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses a linker of contrast correctly?
options: Some people, pain a teacher, I disagree because soften lesson.
Some people will say pain teacher; I disagree, although soften lesson.
Some people will say that pain is a teacher and that I should not soften the lesson. I disagree.
Pain is a teacher some people will say. Disagree I, however.
mcQuestions.explanation: Cleanly state the objection, then respond in a new sentence with 'I disagree'.

options: If anyone simply had said, 'It's not about you.'
If anyone had said simply, 'It's not about you.'
Simply if anyone had said, 'It's not about you.'
If anyone had simply said, 'It's not about you.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Simply' sits between the auxiliary 'had' and the past participle 'said'.
mcQuestions.hint: Adverb between the auxiliary and the participle.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Would your younger self actually listen to this advice if you delivered it now? If not, what would you say instead?
